name: Moros - La Cerrada - Peirón de San Roque - Moros — circular by the SL-Z 22
name_original: Moros - La Cerrada - Peirón de San Roque - Moros — circular por el SL-Z 22
region_intro: Hiking around Moros, Spain, offers a variety of trails through a landscape characterized by rolling hills and agricultural areas, often featuring historical elements. The region provides opportunities to explore local landmarks and natural formations, including ravines and traditional paths. Trails frequently connect villages and offer views of the surrounding countryside. Best hiking trails around Moros The most popular hiking route is Castles and Cross of Armantes — circular via the Barranco del Salto, an 11.2 miles (18.0 km) trail that takes 5 hours 5 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores historical sites and a notable ravine. Another top favourite among local hikers is Moros - La Cerrada - Peirón de San Roque - Moros — circular by the SL-Z 22, an easy 3.1 miles (5.0 km) path. This circular route passes through local landmarks and offers views of the surrounding area. Local hikers also love the Alto Navarro – circular desde Cihuela, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 51 minutes. Hiking around Moros is defined by historical paths, agricultural landscapes, and local hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Moros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 40 hikers have used komoot to explore Moros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Moros?
A: There are over 10 hiking trails available around Moros, offering a variety of experiences through the region's unique landscapes. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars.

Q: What are the typical landscapes I'll encounter while hiking in Moros?
A: Hiking around Moros, Spain, you'll discover a landscape characterized by rolling hills and agricultural areas, often featuring historical elements. Trails frequently connect villages and offer views of the surrounding countryside, including ravines and traditional paths. You might also encounter unique rock formations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5303856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Far West Bilbilitano Rock Formations&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Moros?
A: Yes, Moros offers several easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979485014/moros-la-cerrada-peiron-de-san-roque-moros-circular-by-the-sl-z-22" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moros - La Cerrada - Peirón de San Roque - Moros — circular by the SL-Z 22&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.1-mile path that passes through local landmarks.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Moros?
A: Many of the easier routes in Moros are suitable for families. These trails often feature gentle terrain and shorter distances, allowing for an enjoyable outing with children. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979485014/moros-la-cerrada-peiron-de-san-roque-moros-circular-by-the-sl-z-22" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moros - La Cerrada - Peirón de San Roque - Moros — circular by the SL-Z 22&lt;/a&gt; is a good example of an easy, circular route that families might enjoy.

Q: What interesting sights or landmarks can I explore while hiking around Moros?
A: While hiking around Moros, you can explore various historical and natural landmarks. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5303856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Far West Bilbilitano Rock Formations&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247466" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Armantes&lt;/a&gt; summit, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5325938" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mudéjar Tower of the Church of Santa María de Ateca&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4394218" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ateca&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4394230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrijo de la Cañada&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there good viewpoints on the trails in Moros?
A: Yes, several trails in Moros offer scenic viewpoints. The region's rolling hills and elevated paths provide opportunities for panoramic views of the agricultural landscapes and surrounding countryside. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247466" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Armantes&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent summit, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5303848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillejos Viewpoint (Sierra de Armantes)&lt;/a&gt; is specifically noted for its vistas.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Moros?
A: Yes, Moros offers several circular hiking routes. These are great for exploring the area without retracing your steps. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e972092550/castles-and-cross-of-armantes-circular-via-the-barranco-del-salto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castles and Cross of Armantes — circular via the Barranco del Salto&lt;/a&gt;, which is a more challenging 11.2-mile loop.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Moros?
A: The trails in Moros are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 9 reviews. Hikers often praise the historical paths, varied terrain, and the opportunity to explore local landmarks and natural formations.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Moros?
A: While specific parking details for each trailhead are not always listed, Moros is a rural area, and it is generally possible to find parking in or near the villages where trails begin. We recommend checking the individual tour pages on komoot for more precise information on starting points and potential parking areas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Moros?
A: The best time for hiking in Moros, Spain, generally aligns with the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder and the landscape is vibrant. Summer can be quite warm, while winter offers a different, potentially cooler, experience. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to eat along the hiking routes in Moros?
A: Many hiking routes in Moros connect or pass through local villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4394218" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ateca&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4394230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrijo de la Cañada&lt;/a&gt;. These settlements often have small cafes or restaurants where you can refuel. For longer routes, it's always advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks.
tips:
Good views from the San Blas viewpoint to see the Moorish town along the Manubles River that give us a beautiful picture.
We can park the car at the El Mirador bar and easily access the viewpoint.
Views of the set of reddish rock towers located under the Cerrado pass that we can see from the path next to the Manubles River
Beautiful image that the narrow river Manubles gives us when it is crossed near the Barranco de las Parras

waypoints.name: View of Moros from Mirador de San Blas
